linkedin_org_description:
Arable is leading the global advancement of sustainable agriculture by transforming agronomic decision-making through cutting-edge technology. Forward-thinking organizations in over 50 countries rely on Arable to help them be more productive and sustainable.

The company’s intuitive solution integrates state-of-the-art IoT technology, machine learning, and advanced modeling to convert in-field weather, crop, soil, and irrigation data into real-time, actionable insights. Users are empowered to make informed, proactive decisions that reduce risk and optimize agricultural practices.

Focused on shaping the industry's future, Arable powers transformative initiatives such as large-scale irrigation optimization that conserve hundreds of millions of gallons of water, and agricultural weather intelligence networks supporting thousands of underserved smallholder farmers.

Arable is venture-backed by Galvanize Climate Solutions, Qualcomm Ventures, Prelude, S2G, Ajax Strategies, Grupo Jacto, Middleland, M2O, and iSelect.

For more information, visit www.arable.com.

title: Clinical Pharmacist, Patient & Practice Solutions
description_text:
The clinical Pharmacist of Patient & Practice Solutions will be responsible for leading the transformation of practices “upstream” by implementing a hybrid model of care blending clinical intervention with operational consulting. Supporting specific regional market field teams, practices, and patients, the pharmacist drives performance for medication adherence and other pharmacy-related measures.

By engaging directly with patients, the pharmacist serves as a seamless extension of the practice care team, fostering trust and continuity. This direct patient interaction provides critical feedback, allowing the pharmacist to translate real-world patient barriers into actionable systemic workflow improvements for the practice.

The pharmacist will leverage this unique, holistic perspective and subject matter expertise to diagnose drivers of success and refine strategies for improvement. The ideal candidate is highly adaptable and thrives in dynamic environments, with a proven ability to define processes and structure work within evolving priorities. As the pharmacist will be involved in pilots and "test and learn" workflows, an ability to be nimble is key.

Candidates should be comfortable working remotely/work from home anywhere within the US.


Please note: Valid pharmacist license in at least 2 of the following markets and a willingness to acquire more states as needed: VA, MS, AL, LA, AR, TN, TX, GA, SC, FL




Primary Duties:
\n

Practice and Market Coaching
Engage with high-priority practices to drive quality improvement and service adoption. 
Identify practice barriers, suggesting sustainable and scalable workflow changes, and educating staff. 
Leverage current data to understand individual practice barriers and stakeholder management with relationship building with practices and internal market and field members. 

Patient Engagement
Conduct direct patient engagement and chart review and address patient-specific barriers to medication adherence and disease state management. 
Prevent and resolve medication-related problems and make appropriate evidence-based, patient-centered medication and therapy recommendations back to practice care teams. 
Use insights gained from patient outreach to identify and translate systemic gaps in practice workflows. 

Strategy & Optimization
Leverage clinical expertise and data to support business needs to refine and create standardized resources and tooling to improve medication outcomes for practices, clinicians, and patients. 
Identify drivers of success and refine strategies for improvement.
Participate in pilot programs to "test and learn" workflows
Other duties as assigned 

Minimum Qualifications:
PharmD from an ACPE-approved college of Pharmacy
Active, unrestricted license to practice pharmacy in, at least, two states of practice within assigned region; Willingness to obtain additional licenses if needed
3 yrs experience in value-based care, community or clinical pharmacy practice, or completion of a PGY1 Pharmacy Residency
In depth knowledge of medication adherence, pharmacy quality measures, and medication spend
Proven ability to build relationships with providers and educate patients 
Strong critical thinking & analytical skills
Proficient in Excel or Google Sheets; Ability to learn new technology 
Highly adaptable with the ability to structure work within evolving priorities
Ability to organize and articulate pharmacy performance to market and senior leaders
Valid pharmacist license in at least 2 of the following markets and a willingness to acquire more states as needed: VA, MS, AL, LA, AR, TN, TX, GA, SC, FL

Preferred Qualifications:
Project management experience

Physical Requirements:
Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

\n
$96,000 - $135,000 a year
Salary Range: $96,000 to $135,000 a year + bonus + equity
Compensation for the role will depend on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience.
\n
Who We Are:
Aledade, a public benefit corporation, exists to empower the most transformational part of our health care landscape - independent primary care. We were founded in 2014, and since then, we've become the largest network of independent primary care in the country - helping practices, health centers and clinics deliver better care to their patients and thrive in value-based care. Additionally, by creating value-based contracts across a wide variety of health plans, we aim to flip the script on the traditional fee-for-service model. Our work strengthens continuity of care, aligns incentives and ensures primary care physicians are paid for what they do best - keeping patients healthy. If you want to help create a health care system that is good for patients, good for practices and good for society - and if you're eager to join a collaborative, inclusive and remote-first culture - you've come to the right place.

What Does This Mean for You?
At Aledade, you will be part of a creative culture that is driven by a passion for tackling complex issues with respect, open-mindedness and a desire to learn. You will collaborate with team members who bring a wide range of experiences, interests, backgrounds, beliefs and achievements to their work - and who are all united by a shared passion for public health and a commitment to the Aledade mission.

In addition to time off to support work-life balance and enjoyment, we offer the following comprehensive benefits package designed for the overall well-being of our team members:

Flexible work schedules and the ability to work remotely are available for many roles
Health, dental and vision insurance paid up to 80% for employees, dependents and domestic partners
Robust time-off plan (21 days of PTO in your first year)
Two paid volunteer days and 11 paid holidays
12 weeks paid parental leave for all new parents
Six weeks paid sabbatical after six years of service
Educational Assistant Program and Clinical Employee Reimbursement Program
401(k) with up to 4% match
Stock options
And much more!

At Aledade, we don’t just accept differences, we celebrate them! We strive to attract, develop and retain highly qualified individuals representing the diverse communities where we live and work. Aledade is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Employment policies and decisions at Aledade are based on merit, qualifications, performance and business needs. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth or medical conditions related to pregnancy or childbirth), gender identity or expression, religion, physical or mental disability, medical condition, legally protected genetic information, marital status, veteran status, or sexual orientation.

Privacy Policy: By applying for this job, you agree to Aledade's Applicant Privacy Policy available at https://www.aledade.com/privacy-policy-applicants

They will also identify practice barriers and translate patient feedback into actionable improvements.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a PharmD from an ACPE-approved college of Pharmacy and an active pharmacist license in at least two states. They should also have three years of experience in value-based care or clinical pharmacy practice.

Aledade’s data, personal coaching, user-friendly workflows, health care policy expertise, strong payer relationships and integrated care solutions enable primary care organizations to succeed financially by keeping people healthy. Together with more than 2,400 practices and community health centers in 46 states and the District of Columbia, Aledade manages accountable care organizations that share in the risk and reward across more than 200 value-based contracts representing nearly 3 million patient lives. To learn more, visit www.aledade.com.

We were founded in 2014, and since then, we've become the largest network of independent primary care in the country - helping practices, health centers and clinics deliver better care to their patients and thrive in value-based care. Additionally, by creating value-based contracts across a wide variety of health plans, we aim to flip the script on the traditional fee-for-service model. Our work strengthens continuity of care, aligns incentives and ensures primary care physicians are paid for what they do best - keeping patients healthy. If you want to help create a health care system that is good for patients, good for practices and good for society - and if you're eager to join a collaborative, inclusive and remote-first culture - you've come to the right place.

What Does This Mean for You?
At Aledade, you will be part of a creative culture that is driven by a passion for tackling complex issues with respect, open-mindedness and a desire to learn. You will collaborate with team members who bring a wide range of experiences, interests, backgrounds, beliefs and achievements to their work - and who are all united by a shared passion for public health and a commitment to the Aledade mission.

In addition to time off to support work-life balance and enjoyment, we offer the following comprehensive benefits package designed for the overall well-being of our team members:

Flexible work schedules and the ability to work remotely are available for many roles
Health, dental and vision insurance paid up to 80% for employees, dependents and domestic partners
Robust time-off plan (21 days of PTO in your first year)
Two paid volunteer days and 11 paid holidays
12 weeks paid parental leave for all new parents
Six weeks paid sabbatical after six years of service
Educational Assistant Program and Clinical Employee Reimbursement Program
401(k) with up to 4% match
Stock options
And much more!

At Aledade, we don’t just accept differences, we celebrate them! We strive to attract, develop and retain highly qualified individuals representing the diverse communities where we live and work. Aledade is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Employment policies and decisions at Aledade are based on merit, qualifications, performance and business needs. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth or medical conditions related to pregnancy or childbirth), gender identity or expression, religion, physical or mental disability, medical condition, legally protected genetic information, marital status, veteran status, or sexual orientation.

title: Clinical Pharmacist, Patient & Practice Solutions
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
The clinical Pharmacist of Patient & Practice Solutions will be responsible for leading the transformation of practices “upstream” by implementing a hybrid model of care blending clinical intervention with operational consulting. Supporting specific regional market field teams, practices, and patients, the pharmacist drives performance for medication adherence and other pharmacy-related measures.

By engaging directly with patients, the pharmacist serves as a seamless extension of the practice care team, fostering trust and continuity. This direct patient interaction provides critical feedback, allowing the pharmacist to translate real-world patient barriers into actionable systemic workflow improvements for the practice.

The pharmacist will leverage this unique, holistic perspective and subject matter expertise to diagnose drivers of success and refine strategies for improvement. The ideal candidate is highly adaptable and thrives in dynamic environments, with a proven ability to define processes and structure work within evolving priorities. As the pharmacist will be involved in pilots and "test and learn" workflows, an ability to be nimble is key.

Candidates should be comfortable working remotely/work from home anywhere within the US.


Please note: Valid pharmacist license in at least 2 of the following markets and a willingness to acquire more states as needed: NC, WV, KY, PA, DE, MD, CT, NJ, MD, NY, NH, ME




Primary Duties:
\n

Practice and Market Coaching
Engage with high-priority practices to drive quality improvement and service adoption. 
Identify practice barriers, suggesting sustainable and scalable workflow changes, and educating staff. 
Leverage current data to understand individual practice barriers and stakeholder management with relationship building with practices and internal market and field members. 

Patient Engagement
Conduct direct patient engagement and chart review and address patient-specific barriers to medication adherence and disease state management. 
Prevent and resolve medication-related problems and make appropriate evidence-based, patient-centered medication and therapy recommendations back to practice care teams. 
Use insights gained from patient outreach to identify and translate systemic gaps in practice workflows. 

Strategy & Optimization
Leverage clinical expertise and data to support business needs to refine and create standardized resources and tooling to improve medication outcomes for practices, clinicians, and patients. 
Identify drivers of success and refine strategies for improvement.
Participate in pilot programs to "test and learn" workflows
Other duties as assigned 

Minimum Qualifications:
PharmD from an ACPE-approved college of Pharmacy
Active, unrestricted license to practice pharmacy in, at least, two states of practice within assigned region; Willingness to obtain additional licenses if needed
3 yrs experience in value-based care, community or clinical pharmacy practice, or completion of a PGY1 Pharmacy Residency
In depth knowledge of medication adherence, pharmacy quality measures, and medication spend
Proven ability to build relationships with providers and educate patients 
Strong critical thinking & analytical skills
Proficient in Excel or Google Sheets; Ability to learn new technology 
Highly adaptable with the ability to structure work within evolving priorities
Ability to organize and articulate pharmacy performance to market and senior leaders
Valid pharmacist license in at least 2 of the following markets and a willingness to acquire more states as needed: NC, WV, KY, PA, DE, MD, CT, NJ, MD, NY, NH, ME

Preferred Qualifications:
Project management experience

Physical Requirements:
Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

\n
$96,000 - $135,000 a year
Salary Range: $96,000 to $135,000 a year + bonus + equity
Compensation for the role will depend on a number of factors, including a candidate’s qualifications, skills, competencies and experience.
\n

Aledade, a public benefit corporation, exists to empower the most transformational part of our health care landscape - independent primary care. Our work strengthens continuity of care, aligns incentives and ensures primary care physicians are paid for what they do best - keeping patients healthy. If you want to help create a health care system that is good for patients, good for practices and good for society - and if you're eager to join a collaborative, inclusive and remote-first culture - you've come to the right place.

What Does This Mean for You?

You will collaborate with team members who bring a wide range of experiences, interests, backgrounds, beliefs and achievements to their work - and who are all united by a shared passion for public health and a commitment to the Aledade mission.

title: Member of Growth, Financial Institutions Group (FIG)
description_text:
At Anchorage Digital, we are building the world’s most advanced digital asset platform for institutions to participate in crypto.

Anchorage Digital is a crypto platform that enables institutions to participate in digital assets through custody, staking, trading, governance, settlement, and the industry's leading security infrastructure. Home to Anchorage Digital Bank N.A., the first federally chartered crypto bank in the U.S., Anchorage Digital also serves institutions through Anchorage Digital Singapore, Porto by Anchorage Digital, and other offerings.

The company is funded by leading institutions including Andreessen Horowitz, GIC, Goldman Sachs, KKR, and Visa, with its Series D valuation over $3 billion. Founded in 2017 in San Francisco, California, Anchorage Digital has offices in New York, New York; Porto, Portugal; Singapore; and Sioux Falls, South Dakota. Learn more at anchorage.com, on X @Anchorage, and on LinkedIn. 

This is a unique opportunity to join a high-growth business within a crypto-native company, serving some of the largest, most complex traditional financial institutions as they enter the digital asset space and develop products for themselves and their customers. You will be a critical team member, supporting the department and business line leads as they engage with clients, refine the strategic direction of the business, and collaborate with partners across Anchorage. If you are motivated, hungry to learn, and thrive in a fast and sometimes ambiguous environment - this is the role for you.

We have created the Factors of Growth & Impact to help Villagers better measure impact and articulate coaching, feedback, and the rich and rewarding learning that happens while exploring, developing, and mastering the capabilities and contributions within and outside of the Growth Analyst, FIG role:
\n

Technical Skills:
Direct work experience in traditional financial services businesses, including banking, asset management, capital markets, or wealth management.
Working knowledge of the risk, compliance, legal, and regulatory considerations related to the FIG business and target client base.
Strong research, analytical, writing, presentation, and public communication skills.

Complexity and Impact of Work:
Conduct primary research and data analysis to support and report on strategic initiatives across FIG and within specific business lines or client segments.
Support and manage cross-functional projects, representing FIG with other business lines and departments.
Participate in existing and potential client conversations to develop relationships, promote business growth, and progress client deals.
Collaborate across teams, such as Sales, Marketing, Legal, Risk, Compliance, Operations, and Product to solve problems and define strategy, prioritization, and roadmap execution.
Supports strategy execution and internal FIG operations.

Organizational Knowledge:
Contributes to the prioritization and development of key business initiatives, associated product feature needs, and automation and scalability.
Comfortable in a highly dynamic, growth-oriented environment
Ensures knowledge sharing across FIG.

Communication and Influence:
Contribute to strategic proposals, client-facing presentations, business plans, regular or ad hoc reporting needs and execution roadmaps.
Work within and across FIG and cross-functional boundaries to gain consensus, identify and solution issues, and present to the FIG business leads, Department Head, and stakeholders across Anchorage.

You may be a fit for this role if you have: 
(4-10) years of experience across traditional financial services.
1 year of experience in an institutional focused crypto business, with direct exposure to trading, settlements and/or custody infrastructure is a bonus.
A track record of managing cross-functional initiatives tied to institutional product and service delivery.
Comfort engaging with institutional client leads and engaging multiple internal stakeholders.
Strong communication and coordination instincts, research, data analysis, and presentation chops.
Knowledge of computer science principles and/or basic programing skills (Python, R, Java, SQL, etc) .

Although not a requirement, bonus points if: 
You were emotionally moved by the soundtrack to Hamilton, which chronicles the founding of a new financial system. :)

\n
About Anchorage Digital: Who we are

The Anchorage Village, what we call our team, brings together the brightest minds from platform security, financial services, and distributed ledger technology to provide the building blocks that empower institutions to safely participate in the evolving digital asset ecosystem. As a diverse team of more than 600 members, we are united in one common goal: building the future of finance by providing the foundation upon which value moves safely in the new global economy. 

Anchorage Digital is committed to being a welcoming and inclusive workplace for everyone, and we are intentional about making sure people feel respected, supported, and connected at work—regardless of who you are or where you come from. We value and celebrate our differences and we believe being open about who we are allows us to do the best work of our lives.
Anchorage Digital is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or veteran status. Anchorage Digital considers qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with other legal requirements. “Anchorage Digital” refers to services that are offered either through Anchorage Digital Bank National Association, an OCC-chartered national trust bank, or Anchorage Lending CA, LLC a finance lender licensed by the California Department of Financial Protection and Innovation, License No. 60DBO-11976, or Anchorage Digital Singapore Pte Ltd, a Singapore private limited company, all wholly-owned subsidiaries of Anchor Labs, Inc., a Delaware corporation.

Protecting your privacy rights is important to Anchorage Digital, and we work to maintain the trust and confidence of our clients when handling personal or financial information. Please see our privacy policy notices here.

title: IT Automation Engineer
description_text:
As our IT Automation Engineer, you won't just be managing tickets or performing routine maintenance—you'll be an architect of efficiency, tasked with transforming our manual IT processes into a streamlined, automated ecosystem. You will have the autonomy to design and build the workflows that define how we scale. This isn't just about writing scripts; it's about shaping the future of our digital workplace and seeing your code directly reduce the daily burden on your peers and the broader organization.
\n

On a typical day, you might:
Architect and build complex workflows that automate repetitive tasks across our entire stack of enterprise applications.
Create intelligent routing and auto-remediation systems to proactively solve issues, significantly reducing the L1/L2 support burden.
Implement AI-powered workflows to handle high-stakes tasks like access reviews, compliance checks, and intelligent alerting.
Deploy self-service portals that empower employees to solve problems independently, enhancing the overall user experience.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Security and GRC teams to integrate compliance automations directly into our infrastructure.
Orchestrate identity lifecycles using Okta workflows and SCIM provisioning to ensure seamless and secure access.
Troubleshoot and refine integration points when automation logic meets real-world complexity, ensuring our systems remain resilient.

The ideal candidate will bring:
3+ years of hands-on experience managing enterprise IT systems with a dedicated focus on automation over manual operations.
Advanced Python development skills, with a proven ability to write maintainable, production-grade code that includes robust error handling.
Expertise in IdP administration, specifically regarding lifecycle automation, SCIM provisioning, and complex workflow orchestration.
Deep familiarity with MDM platforms (such as Kandji, Intune, or Jamf) and a commitment to zero-touch deployment strategies.
Technical proficiency with APIs and webhooks, allowing you to programmatically integrate and extend various SaaS platforms.
A background in AI-driven automation, including experience building workflows using LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) for intelligent decision-making.
Experience with compliance or security operations, ideally with an infrastructure-as-code mindset and an understanding of zero-trust networking.
Strong collaborative abilities to translate complex business requirements from Security, GRC, and Engineering into elegant technical solutions.
Experience managing Google Workspace at scale preferred, utilizing advanced admin features to optimize the employee experience.
​A strong background in DevOps or security operations is a bonus, for approaching IT challenges with an infrastructure-as-code mindset to ensure scalability and consistency.

\n
$38.08 - $46.54 an hour
This base hourly range is equivalent to an annualized base compensation range of $79,205 - $96,806, assuming you worked a full-time schedule for the entire year (2080 hours). Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base rate only and do not include bonus, equity, sales incentives or other role specific compensation that the role may be eligible for. ActiveCampaign believes in and is committed to equitable compensation practices. The salary range provided above is a good faith estimate of the pay range determined by the location associated with the job posting. The actual salary depends on a candidate’s skills, experience, and work location.
\n
About ActiveCampaign:
ActiveCampaign is the autonomous marketing platform for people at the heart of the action. It empowers teams to automate their campaigns with AI agents that imagine, activate, and validate–freeing them from step-by-step workflows and unlocking limitless ways to orchestrate their marketing. 

With AI, goal-based automation, and 1,000+ app integrations, agencies, marketers, and owners can build cross-channel campaigns in minutes–fine-tuned with billions of data points to drive real results for their unique business.

ActiveCampaign is the trusted choice to help businesses unlock a new world of boundless opportunities–where ideas become impact and potential turns into real results.

As a global multicultural company, we are proud of our inclusive culture which embraces diverse voices, backgrounds, and perspectives. We don’t just celebrate our differences, we believe our diversity is what empowers our innovation and success. You can find out more about our DEI initiatives here. 

Perks and benefits:
At ActiveCampaign, we prioritize employees’ well-being and professional growth by cultivating a culture centered on collaboration and innovation. When you join our team, you’ll not only have the opportunity to make a significant impact, but also enjoy a range of benefits tailored to support your personal and career development. 

Here are some of the benefits we offer:
-Comprehensive Health & Wellness: Top-tier benefits package that includes a fully-covered High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP), complimentary access to telehealth services, and a free subscription to Calm.
-Growth & Development: Access to LinkedIn Learning, professional development programs, and career growth opportunities in a fast-growing organization.
-Generous Paid Time Off: Recharge and take the time you need to maintain work-life balance with open PTO. 
-Total Rewards: Generous 401(k) matching with immediate vesting, quarterly perks with commuter and lunch benefits for hub based employees or a stipend for remote workers, and a four-week paid sabbatical with bonus after five years.
-Collaborative Culture: Work alongside brilliant, passionate colleagues in an environment that values innovation, teamwork, and mutual support.

ActiveCampaign is an equal opportunity employer. We recruit, hire, pay, grow and promote no matter of gender, race, color, sexual orientation, religion, age, protected veteran status, physical and mental abilities, or any other identities protected by law. 

Our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) strive to foster a diverse inclusive environment by supporting each other, building a strong sense of belonging, and creating opportunities for mentorship and professional growth for their members.

title: Physical Therapist *Flexible Schedule* Mobile Outpatient - Revere, MA
description_text:
About Luna 
Founded in 2018, Luna has quickly emerged as the leading in-home tech-enabled physical therapy platform. Over 3000 PTs choose Luna to empower their practice by avoiding burnout and enabling renewed hope in their profession. Luna is proud to partner with outstanding organizations like Emory Health, Powerback, HopCo, Providence, Evergreen, Scripps, UCLA, AARP, and more - further solidifying our commitment to exceptional healthcare delivery. Learn more at www.getluna.com or simply apply here to express interest in speaking with a clinician from our therapist acquisition team! 

Job Description 

We are looking for experienced and collaborative clinicians to provide outpatient PT services to patients in their homes in the Revere area (and other nearby locations). This is outpatient therapy delivered to the patient's home, NOT typical home health. As a physical therapist, your job is fully centered around helping others. Often we find that PTs feel burnout and exhaustion from their career path. There has to be a better way, and it takes technology, community, and equitable compensation. 

We offer flexibility, work-life balance and pay daily
Therapists succeed when they are given the freedom and independence to do what they do best. That’s why our model allows you to choose when you want to see patients during any hours/days of the week. Luna enables you to decide. Do you need a full schedule of patients, or just to supplement your standing clinic hours? Luna offers the platform to create just what you need. 

We provide competitive compensation, Concierge service, and technology you won’t get anywhere else
Alongside competitive compensation, regular bonuses, and instant payment, we've developed support and technology with the physical therapist's well-being at the top of our minds. From safety, cancellations, and billing, to finding the patient's home or facility; unique issues can make the independent therapist's job more challenging than expected. 

\n

Built for PTs and their well-being 
No productivity standards
More work = more earnings
Enjoy increased autonomy with unmatched support 
Eliminate burnout and reclaim your love for the profession!

Reimagining a tech-enabled physical therapy experience 
Luna Auto-Charting automates the time-consuming process of documentation
Luna's Safety Shield for a convenient and safe experience for our PTs 
Luna Exercises to deliver an integrated care experience by prescribing and monitoring workouts via the Luna app
Geofencing technology to match patients based on your specialty and location (within 30 minute drive) 
Instantly receive payments that are direct deposited and visible in-app!

Job Requirements
State-specific PT license
Reliable, personal transportation for therapists practicing outside city centers
1+ years of experience
Liability insurance
CPR/BLS

Join the Lunaverse!
Job Types: Part-time, Contract, Per diem
Benefits: Flexible Scheduling, Referral Program
Medical specialties: Geriatrics, Home Health, Neurology, Orthopedics, Physical & Rehabilitation Medicine, Sports Medicine
Schedule: Choose your own hours
Work Location: On the road

\n
$70 - $75 an hour
In addition to regular bonuses. 
\n
Luna Values

Care Exceptionally 
We value safety and trust even when mistakes occur. We prioritize open and purposeful communication, addressing concerns early and often. We apply creative and innovative efforts aimed at enabling the success of our patients, therapists, physicians, partners, and team members.
Incredibly Relentless 
We prioritize persistence even over immediate success. We understand that continuous effort often leads to greater long-term achievements. We empower each other to make decisions, and approach our goals deliberately and holistically.
Be Impactful
We prioritize fewer high-impact goals over lots of low-impact goals. We are decisive, yet keep an open-mind. We assume positive intent, and hold one another accountable. We know that the details matter, and take ownership in solutions that prioritize the patient and therapist experience.
Become 1% Better, Every Day
We value continual growth over the professional "comfort zone." We have a culture of curiosity and an obsession for constant improvement. We believe there's always a better way to do things. We celebrate our successes and learn from our failures.

Learn more at www.getluna.com or simply apply here to express interest in speaking with a clinician from our therapist acquisition team! 

Job Description 

We are looking for experienced and collaborative clinicians to provide outpatient PT services to patients in their homes in the Peoria, AZ area (and other nearby locations). This is outpatient therapy delivered to the patient's home, NOT typical home health. As a physical therapist, your job is fully centered around helping others. Often we find that PTs feel burnout and exhaustion from their career path. There has to be a better way. It takes technology, community, and equitable compensation. 

We offer autonomy, flexibility, & work-life balance
Therapists succeed when they are given the freedom and independence to do what they do best. That’s why our model allows you to choose when you want to see patients during any hours/days of the week. Care Exceptionally 
We value safety and trust even when mistakes occur. We prioritize open and purposeful communication, addressing concerns early and often. We apply creative and innovative efforts aimed at enabling the success of our patients, therapists, physicians, partners, and team members.
Incredibly Relentless 
We prioritize persistence even over immediate success. We understand that continuous effort often leads to greater long-term achievements. We empower each other to make decisions, and approach our goals deliberately and holistically.

We prioritize fewer high-impact goals over lots of low-impact goals. We are decisive, yet keep an open-mind. We assume positive intent, and hold one another accountable. We know that the details matter, and take ownership in solutions that prioritize the patient and therapist experience.
Become 1% Better, Every Day

title: Pilot Plant Engineering Co-op
description_text:
Sublime is advancing a breakthrough, clean, efficient, low-waste process for manufacturing cement, the essential ingredient in the most-consumed human-made material on Earth: concrete. Replacing 200-year-old manufacturing methods that are reliant on kilns running hotter than lava, our near-ambient-temperature, electrochemical technology manufactures industry-accepted cement and critical minerals. We’re partnering with leading general contractors, concrete producers, developers, and technology hyperscalers to deploy our clean, durable, beautiful Sublime Cement® in infrastructure that is defining the future of clean construction. Backed by some of the world’s largest cement producers, leading private investors, and the U.S. Department of Energy, we are assembling a talented, collaborative team that is "amped about cement" (pun intended) and committed to operating on the frontiers of material science, manufacturing, industry, construction, and commercial partnerships, to advance cement innovation that will endure for the next millennium.

Position Overview 

Sublime Systems is seeking a creative and motivated Engineering Co-op to support the dynamic operations of the Sublime Systems pilot plant. The pilot plant is in a key position within the company, scaling up R&D technologies in preparation for commercialization while also producing significant quantities of Sublime materials for further research, development, and use. In this role, you will learn to operate our pilot plant using an HMI, take and analyze process samples, and aid in the collection of data as we develop and scale up processes. You will also help to maintain pilot equipment, implement process improvements, and execute experiments to inform scale-up decisions. 
This role is expected to be hands-on, with a significant amount of time spent engaging in pilot operation and troubleshooting. Successful candidates will be results-driven, ambitious, organized, and able to thrive in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities. 

The ideal candidate will have: 

- A strong sense for chemical safety, organization, and attention to detail 
- Previous internship and/or lab experience 
- An eagerness for hands-on work and for learning new processes and unit operations 
- The ability to make keen observations on process anomalies, recommend process improvements, and articulate findings 

This is a paid full-time (40 hr/wk) in-person position located in Somerville, MA. 

\n

Responsibilities Include: 
Daily operation of the pilot system, controlled with a PLC system through an HMI 
Perform necessary pre-operation and end-of-shift activities to ensure proper equipment startup and operation on multiple pieces of equipment. This will include reactors, pumps, agitators, filter presses, etc. 
Participate in the sample preparation, analysis, and interpretation of lab- and pilot-scale data 
Participate in downstream product processing 
Document process operations 
Perform hands-on troubleshooting and repair of pilot equipment 
Perform routine investigative teardowns of process equipment to capture physical materials characteristics 
Assist in the design and documentation of new pilot projects 
Facilitate installation and assembly of new equipment 
Maintain pilot plant conditions in a fashion that facilitates cleanliness and productivity 
Move reasonably sized drums of chemicals and packages of raw materials by hand or using a hand pallet jack (no forklift operation) 

Knowledge and Skill Requirements 
Actively pursuing a degree in Mechanical, Chemical, or Industrial Engineering or a related field 
Knowledge of and passion for applying engineering fundamentals to chemical plant design and operation 
Able to lift objects up to 50lbs, climb ladders, and work on step stools 
Ability to pay close attention to detail, focusing on accuracy and quality, without losing sight of the bigger picture 
Balance, perspective, and a good sense of humor 

\n
$25 - $25 an hour
\n
Sublime Systems is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Your Right to Work
In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification document form upon hire.

Notice of E-Verify
Sublime Systems use E-Verify to confirm the identity and employment eligibility of all new hires.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves daily operation of the pilot system, including pre-operation and end-of-shift activities, sample preparation, and troubleshooting of pilot equipment. Additionally, the co-op will assist in the design and documentation of new pilot projects and maintain cleanliness and productivity in the pilot plant.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should be actively pursuing a degree in Mechanical, Chemical, or Industrial Engineering or a related field, with a strong sense for chemical safety and attention to detail. Previous internship or lab experience is preferred, along with the ability to lift objects up to 50lbs and work on ladders.
linkedin_org_slogan: Clean cement for a resilient future
linkedin_org_locations: 444 Somerville Ave, Somerville, Massachusetts 02143, US
linkedin_org_description:
Sublime Systems is leading a swift and massive transition to a clean cement manufacturing future. 

Sublime’s breakthrough electrochemical process avoids the pollution caused by ordinary portland cement’s use of fossil-fueled kilns and limestone feedstock. Sublime instead extracts reactive calcium and silicates from abundant raw materials at ambient temperature, to make ASTM C1157-compliant Sublime Cement™, a 1:1 replacement for OPC in concrete. Because it does not require carbon capture, Sublime’s technology offers a clear path for cost-competitive clean cement manufacturing at scale. 

Sublime was founded at MIT in 2020 by Dr. Leah Ellis and Prof. Yet-Ming Chiang, both respected experts in materials science, electrochemical systems, and sustainability research. The company has raisedunding from leading tech investors, global cement majors, and cooperative agreements with the U.S. Department of Energy. 

It currently operates a Somerville, MA-based pilot plant with a >250 TPY nameplate production capacity and is developing its 30,000 TPY first commercial facility in Holyoke, MA.

title: Senior Quality Control Assistant
locations_derived: New York, New York, United States
description_text:
Catbird, a DTC fine jewelry brand, is looking for a Senior QC Assistant to join our team in Brooklyn, NY! 

We are currently seeking a Senior Quality Control Inspector (Level 2) to join our Quality team. This role reports directly to the Quality Control Supervisor and is responsible for the hands‑on inspection of high‑end fine jewelry, including finished pieces and raw materials such as diamonds, pearls, and gemstones.
The ideal candidate is detail‑oriented, technically curious, and comfortable working in a fast‑paced production environment. Under limited supervision, this role performs visual and technical inspections of jewelry produced in‑house and by external vendo

\n

Key Responsibilities 
Provide technical expertise and appropriate industry vocabulary to support and guide cross‑functional partners, including Customer Service, Gold Room, Design & Development, and Production.
Reinforce and manage QC workflow priorities, including floor pickup hours and web delivery schedules, ensuring that stock, bulk production, and special orders are inspected according to due dates and business needs.
Communicate daily with team leads and the Training department regarding jeweler performance, quality trends, and workflow redirection through clear reporting, emails, and Slack communication.
Perform initial inspections of recycled and lab‑grown diamonds submitted by Purchasing using binocular microscopes and precision tools, documenting findings and feedback in Excel reports.
Document inspection results with clear written notes and photographic evidence, entering accurate data into company systems including NetSuite, Piro, and Teamwork.
Execute quality inspections for outsourced vendor production to ensure manufacturing continuity and product excellence. Provide constructive technical feedback, answer vendor questions, and properly route, reject, or release work orders in NetSuite.
Support the training and onboarding of junior QC team members by reinforcing system usage, process steps, inspection standards, and PO creation, helping build confidence and engagement.

Our Ideal Candidtate
Basic to intermediate computer skills required (Excel, internal systems, Slack).
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to deliver professional, constructive feedback.
Ability to work collaboratively and courteously in a high‑volume, time‑sensitive environment.
Strong attention to detail and ability to make timely inspection decisions.
Willingness to continuously learn and expand technical jewelry knowledge.

\n
$27 - $28 an hour
\n

title: Product R&D Co-op
description_text:
Sublime is advancing a breakthrough, clean, efficient, low-waste process for manufacturing cement, the essential ingredient in the most-consumed human-made material on Earth: concrete. Replacing 200-year-old manufacturing methods that are reliant on kilns running hotter than lava, our near-ambient-temperature, electrochemical technology manufactures industry-accepted cement and critical minerals. We’re partnering with leading general contractors, concrete producers, developers, and technology hyperscalers to deploy our clean, durable, beautiful Sublime Cement® in infrastructure that is defining the future of clean construction. Backed by some of the world’s largest cement producers, leading private investors, and the U.S. Department of Energy, we are assembling a talented, collaborative team that is "amped about cement" (pun intended) and committed to operating on the frontiers of material science, manufacturing, industry, construction, and commercial partnerships, to advance cement innovation that will endure for the next millennium.

Position Overview 

Sublime Systems seeks a creative and dynamic R&D Co-op to contribute to the development of our decarbonized cement. The co-op will be responsible for conducting research including hands-on lab-scale testing, data analysis, and literature reviews. As a member of Sublime Systems’ R&D team, the R&D co-op will work closely with the company’s experienced researchers and will receive extensive mentorship. This is an exciting opportunity to combat global climate change by transforming how the world makes cement. The role will focus on the development and testing of Sublime Systems’ decarbonized cement. This role is expected to be hands-on, with a significant amount of time spent engaging in lab testing. The ideal candidate will have experience working in a laboratory, and is results-driven, detail-oriented, organized, and able to thrive in a fast-paced environment. 

This is a paid full-time (40 hr/wk) in-person position located in Somerville, MA. 
\n

Responsibilities Include: 
Performing experiments in the laboratory including preparing and testing samples of Sublime Cement and cementitious materials 
Measuring cement material properties such as heat release, compressive strength, setting time, and flow characteristics in Sublime Systems’ laboratory 
Performing physical or chemical characterization measurements on Sublime materials 
Analyzing data, interpreting results, and presenting findings to Sublime’s team 
Contributing to the development of Sublime’s process through literature reviews 
Championing safety, especially in the development and execution of experimental processes 
Fostering Sublime Systems’ reputation by delivering innovative solutions 
Helping to develop a supportive, innovative, and dynamic company culture 

Knowledge and Skill Requirements 
Pursuing a B.S. in civil engineering, mechanical engineering, materials science or engineering, chemical engineering, materials chemistry, or a related field. 
Strong knowledge of materials science fundamentals, including basic materials chemistry and mechanics of materials concepts. 
Able to perform lab work including preparing cement material test specimens, setting up and operating testing equipment, and recording results. 
Able to lift objects up to 50 lbs. 
Familiarity with physical and chemical characterization techniques such as compression testing, SEM, XRD, XRF, BET, and ICP is a plus. 
Strong presentation and communication skills, both written and verbal. 
Strong interpersonal skills including the ability to listen, distill information, consult, and suggest new approaches. 
Ability to pay close attention to detail, focusing on accuracy and quality, without losing sight of the bigger picture. 
Balance, perspective, and a good sense of humor. 

\n
$25 - $25 an hour
\n
Sublime Systems is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Your Right to Work
In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification document form upon hire.

Notice of E-Verify

Because it does not require carbon capture, Sublime’s technology offers a clear path for cost-competitive clean cement manufacturing at scale. 

Sublime was founded at MIT in 2020 by Dr. Leah Ellis and Prof. Yet-Ming Chiang, both respected experts in materials science, electrochemical systems, and sustainability research. The company has raisedunding from leading tech investors, global cement majors, and cooperative agreements with the U.S. Department of Energy. 

It currently operates a Somerville, MA-based pilot plant with a >250 TPY nameplate production capacity and is developing its 30,000 TPY first commercial facility in Holyoke, MA.

title: Engineering, Solids Co-op
description_text:
Sublime is advancing a breakthrough, clean, efficient, low-waste process for manufacturing cement, the essential ingredient in the most-consumed human-made material on Earth: concrete. Replacing 200-year-old manufacturing methods that are reliant on kilns running hotter than lava, our near-ambient-temperature, electrochemical technology manufactures industry-accepted cement and critical minerals. We’re partnering with leading general contractors, concrete producers, developers, and technology hyperscalers to deploy our clean, durable, beautiful Sublime Cement® in infrastructure that is defining the future of clean construction. Backed by some of the world’s largest cement producers, leading private investors, and the U.S. Department of Energy, we are assembling a talented, collaborative team that is "amped about cement" (pun intended) and committed to operating on the frontiers of material science, manufacturing, industry, construction, and commercial partnerships, to advance cement innovation that will endure for the next millennium.

Position Overview 
This is an exciting opportunity to transform how the world makes cement. Sublime Systems seeks a creative and dynamic Engineering Intern to support operations of our first pilot plant. 
In your role you will learn to operate our pilot plant as well as take and analyze process samples. You will help maintain pilot equipment as well as implement process improvements. 
The ideal candidate will have: 
- A strong sense for safety, organization, and attention to detail. 
- Previous internship and/or lab experience 
- Ability to make keen observations on process anomalies, recommend process improvements, and articulate findings. 

This is a paid full-time (40 hr/wk) in-person position located in Somerville, MA.

\n

Responsibilities Include: 
Daily operation of equipment and testing in Sublime’s crushing/milling pilot system 
Perform necessary pre-operation and end of shift activities to ensure proper equipment start-up and operation on multiple pieces of equipment. This will include driers, crushers, mills, and other similar equipment 
Participate in the sample prep, analysis and interpretation of lab and pilot scale experiments. 
Document process operations 
Facilitate installation and assembly of new equipment. 
Maintain pilot plant conditions in a fashion that facilitates cleanliness and productivity 
Move reasonably sized drums and packages of raw materials by hand or using a hand pallet jack 

Knowledge and Skill Requirements 
Actively pursuing a degree in Mechanical or Industrial engineering or a related field 
Knowledge of and passion for applying engineering fundamentals to chemical plant design and operation 
Able to lift objects up to 50 lb, climb ladders, and work on step stools 
Knowledge and ability to use hand tools such as combination wrenches, impact wrenches, torque meters, etc.
Ability to pay close attention to detail, focusing on accuracy and quality, without losing sight of the bigger picture. 
Balance, perspective, and a good sense of humor. 

\n
$25 - $25 an hour
\n
Sublime Systems is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Your Right to Work
In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification document form upon hire.

Notice of E-Verify
Sublime Systems use E-Verify to confirm the identity and employment eligibility of all new hires.
ai_core_responsibilities: The intern will operate equipment and conduct testing in the pilot plant, as well as participate in sample preparation and analysis. They will also document process operations and help maintain pilot plant conditions.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should be pursuing a degree in Mechanical or Industrial Engineering or a related field and have knowledge of engineering fundamentals. They should also be able to lift objects up to 50 lbs and have experience using hand tools.
linkedin_org_slogan: Clean cement for a resilient future
linkedin_org_locations: 444 Somerville Ave, Somerville, Massachusetts 02143, US
linkedin_org_description:
Sublime Systems is leading a swift and massive transition to a clean cement manufacturing future. 

Sublime’s breakthrough electrochemical process avoids the pollution caused by ordinary portland cement’s use of fossil-fueled kilns and limestone feedstock. Sublime instead extracts reactive calcium and silicates from abundant raw materials at ambient temperature, to make ASTM C1157-compliant Sublime Cement™, a 1:1 replacement for OPC in concrete. Because it does not require carbon capture, Sublime’s technology offers a clear path for cost-competitive clean cement manufacturing at scale. 

Sublime was founded at MIT in 2020 by Dr. Leah Ellis and Prof. Yet-Ming Chiang, both respected experts in materials science, electrochemical systems, and sustainability research. The company has raisedunding from leading tech investors, global cement majors, and cooperative agreements with the U.S. Department of Energy. 

It currently operates a Somerville, MA-based pilot plant with a >250 TPY nameplate production capacity and is developing its 30,000 TPY first commercial facility in Holyoke, MA.

title: Physical Therapist *Flexible Schedule* Mobile Outpatient - Goodyear, AZ
description_text:
About Luna 
Founded in 2018, Luna has quickly emerged as the leading in-home tech-enabled physical therapy platform. Over 3000 PTs choose Luna to empower their practice by avoiding burnout and enabling renewed hope in their profession. Luna is proud to partner with outstanding organizations like Emory Health, Powerback, HopCo, Providence, Evergreen, Scripps, UCLA, AARP, and more - further solidifying our commitment to exceptional healthcare delivery. Learn more at www.getluna.com or simply apply here to express interest in speaking with a clinician from our therapist acquisition team! 

Job Description 

We are looking for experienced and collaborative clinicians to provide outpatient PT services to patients in their homes in the Goodyear, Arizona area (and other nearby locations). This is outpatient therapy delivered to the patient's home, NOT typical home health. As a physical therapist, your job is fully centered around helping others. Often we find that PTs feel burnout and exhaustion from their career path. There has to be a better way. It takes technology, community, and equitable compensation. 

We offer autonomy, flexibility, & work-life balance
Therapists succeed when they are given the freedom and independence to do what they do best. That’s why our model allows you to choose when you want to see patients during any hours/days of the week. Luna enables you to decide. Do you need a full schedule of patients, or just to supplement your standing clinic hours? Luna offers the platform to create just what you need. 

We provide competitive compensation, Concierge service, and technology you won’t get anywhere else
Alongside competitive compensation, regular bonuses, and instant payment, we've developed support and technology with the physical therapist's well-being at the top of our minds. From safety, cancellations, and billing, to finding the patient's home or facility; unique issues can make the independent therapist's job more challenging than expected. 

\n

Built for PTs to focus on what matters most
No productivity standards
More work = more earnings
Enjoy increased autonomy with unmatched support 
Eliminate burnout and reclaim your love for the profession!

Reimagining a tech-enabled physical therapy experience 
Luna Auto-Charting automates the time-consuming process of documentation
Luna's Safety Shield for a convenient and safe experience for our PTs 
Luna Exercises to deliver an integrated care experience by prescribing and monitoring workouts via the Luna app
Geofencing technology to match patients based on your specialty and location (within 30 minute drive) 
Instantly receive payments that are direct deposited and visible in-app!

Job Requirements
State-specific PT license
Reliable, personal transportation for therapists practicing outside city centers
1+ years of experience
Liability insurance
CPR/BLS

Join the Lunaverse!
Job Types: Part-time, Contract, Per diem
Benefits: Flexible Scheduling, Referral Program
Medical specialties: Geriatrics, Home Health, Neurology, Orthopedics, Physical & Rehabilitation Medicine, Sports Medicine
Schedule: Choose your own hours
Work Location: On the road

\n
$70 - $75 an hour
In addition to regular bonuses. Cancellation rates may vary. 
\n
Luna Values

Care Exceptionally 
We value safety and trust even when mistakes occur. We prioritize open and purposeful communication, addressing concerns early and often. We apply creative and innovative efforts aimed at enabling the success of our patients, therapists, physicians, partners, and team members.
Incredibly Relentless 
We prioritize persistence even over immediate success. We understand that continuous effort often leads to greater long-term achievements. We empower each other to make decisions, and approach our goals deliberately and holistically.
Be Impactful
We prioritize fewer high-impact goals over lots of low-impact goals. We are decisive, yet keep an open-mind. We assume positive intent, and hold one another accountable. We know that the details matter, and take ownership in solutions that prioritize the patient and therapist experience.
Become 1% Better, Every Day
We value continual growth over the professional "comfort zone." We have a culture of curiosity and an obsession for constant improvement. We believe there's always a better way to do things. We celebrate our successes and learn from our failures.

title: Process R&D, Scale- up Co-op
description_text:
Sublime is advancing a breakthrough, clean, efficient, low-waste process for manufacturing cement, the essential ingredient in the most-consumed human-made material on Earth: concrete. Replacing 200-year-old manufacturing methods that are reliant on kilns running hotter than lava, our near-ambient-temperature, electrochemical technology manufactures industry-accepted cement and critical minerals. We’re partnering with leading general contractors, concrete producers, developers, and technology hyperscalers to deploy our clean, durable, beautiful Sublime Cement® in infrastructure that is defining the future of clean construction. Backed by some of the world’s largest cement producers, leading private investors, and the U.S. Department of Energy, we are assembling a talented, collaborative team that is "amped about cement" (pun intended) and committed to operating on the frontiers of material science, manufacturing, industry, construction, and commercial partnerships, to advance cement innovation that will endure for the next millennium.

Position Overview 

Sublime Systems is seeking a creative and driven Co-op to assist in the scale up of Sublime’s technology. This is an exciting chance to work on process development both at the research and pilot scales and support the goals of process commercialization. In this role, you will gain experience with small scale reactor design and operation as well as learn how to assemble and operate pilot plant equipment. You will also help to maintain pilot equipment, implement process improvements, and execute experiments to inform scale-up decisions. 
This role is expected to be hands-on, with a significant amount of time spent engaging in pilot operation and troubleshooting. Successful candidates will be results-driven, ambitious, organized, and able to thrive in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities. 
The ideal candidate will have: 

- A strong sense for chemical safety, organization, and attention to detail 
- Previous internship and/or lab experience 
- An eagerness for hands-on work and for learning new processes and unit operations 
- A basic understanding of reactor design and/or material characterization techniques 
This is a paid full-time (40 hr/wk) in-person position located in Somerville, MA. 
\n

Responsibilities Include: 
Daily operation of laboratory-scale chemical reactors 
Routine synthesis and processing of various materials, both powder and liquid 
Interpreting and/or performing physical, chemical, and mechanical characterization of synthesized materials 
Analyzing data, interpreting results, and presenting conclusions to the larger team 
Facilitate planning, installation, and assembly of new equipment 
Move reasonably sized drums of chemicals and packages of raw materials by hand or using a hand pallet jack (no forklift operation) 
Participate in downstream product processing 
Document process operations and participate in safety reviews/planning 
Perform hands-on troubleshooting and repair of pilot equipment 

Knowledge and Skill Requirements 
Actively pursuing a degree in Mechanical, Chemical, or Industrial Engineering or a related field 
Knowledge of and passion for applying engineering fundamentals to chemical process design and operation 
Able to lift objects up to 50lbs, climb ladders, and work on step stools 
Ability to pay close attention to detail, focusing on accuracy and quality, without losing sight of the bigger picture 
Desire to work on new processes and learn new skills on-the-job 
Familiarity with physical, chemical, and mechanical characterization techniques, such as Laser Diffraction, ICP-OES, SEM, XRF, XRD, titrations, UV-Vis, compressive testing, etc. 
Balance, perspective, and a good sense of humor 

\n
$25 - $25 an hour
\n
Sublime Systems is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer
All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Your Right to Work
In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification document form upon hire.

Notice of E-Verify
Sublime Systems use E-Verify to confirm the identity and employment eligibility of all new hires.

Previous internship or lab experience is preferred, along with a strong attention to detail and eagerness for hands-on work.

linkedin_org_description:
Sublime Systems is leading a swift and massive transition to a clean cement manufacturing future. 

Sublime’s breakthrough electrochemical process avoids the pollution caused by ordinary portland cement’s use of fossil-fueled kilns and limestone feedstock. Sublime instead extracts reactive calcium and silicates from abundant raw materials at ambient temperature, to make ASTM C1157-compliant Sublime Cement™, a 1:1 replacement for OPC in concrete. Because it does not require carbon capture, Sublime’s technology offers a clear path for cost-competitive clean cement manufacturing at scale. 

Sublime was founded at MIT in 2020 by Dr. Leah Ellis and Prof. Yet-Ming Chiang, both respected experts in materials science, electrochemical systems, and sustainability research. The company has raisedunding from leading tech investors, global cement majors, and cooperative agreements with the U.S. Department of Energy. 

It currently operates a Somerville, MA-based pilot plant with a >250 TPY nameplate production capacity and is developing its 30,000 TPY first commercial facility in Holyoke, MA.

title: Sales Director
description_text:
The Director, Corporate Sales is responsible for leading and executing all aspects of the sales function, including new business development, revenue and gross profit attainment, and execution of a defined growth strategy across assigned regions. This role provides leadership, mentorship, and performance management to Account Managers, ensuring consistent sales processes, effective prospecting strategies, and high-quality customer engagement.

The Director will collaborate closely with senior leadership to align sales initiatives with overall organizational goals, drive operational excellence, and support long-term growth.

\n

Responsibilities 
Oversee and manage daily corporate sales activities
Establish guidelines for prospecting, client meetings, reporting, and pipeline management
Coach and mentor Account Managers through regular performance meetings and field support
Accompany Account Managers on customer calls to provide guidance and sales coaching
Support and participate in industry events and trade shows
Attend professional training and certification programs as required
Ensure alignment of sales strategy with organizational goals, including performance, retention, and talent development
Prepare, administer, and distribute timely sales reports, forecasts, and projections
Communicate sales trends, risks, and opportunities to the management team
Lead staff development and training initiatives, including ongoing performance evaluations
Develop and implement a comprehensive sales plan, including:
Market research and analysis
Sales projections and forecasting
Territory or vertical market assignments
Key account identification and development strategies
Establish standardized procedures for submitting high-quality customer proposals
Ensure all sales staff evaluations are conducted in accordance with established policies
Attend client or project meetings as needed and determine appropriate escalation or leadership involvement

Requirements
Strong communication skills with the ability to interact effectively with employees, clients, and leadership
Demonstrated leadership and management experience in a collaborative, team-oriented environment
Proven ability to motivate and lead sales and administrative teams toward shared objectives
Self-motivated, results-driven professional capable of working independently and meeting deadlines
Excellent organizational skills with strong attention to detail
Ability to lead or significantly contribute to operational and strategic initiatives
High school diploma or equivalent required
Minimum of 5–7 years of sales management experience, preferably in a technology, systems integration, or related industry


\n
$140,000 - $180,000 a year
\n
#LAT
ai_core_responsibilities: The Sales Director oversees and manages corporate sales activities, ensuring effective prospecting and client engagement. They also coach Account Managers and align sales strategies with organizational goals.

title: Physical Therapist *Flexible Schedule* Mobile Outpatient - Newton, MA
description_text:
About Luna 
Founded in 2018, Luna has quickly emerged as the leading in-home tech-enabled physical therapy platform. Over 3000 PTs choose Luna to empower their practice by avoiding burnout and enabling renewed hope in their profession. Luna is proud to partner with outstanding organizations like Emory Health, Powerback, HopCo, Providence, Evergreen, Scripps, UCLA, AARP, and more - further solidifying our commitment to exceptional healthcare delivery. Learn more at www.getluna.com or simply apply here to express interest in speaking with a clinician from our therapist acquisition team! 

Job Description 

We are looking for experienced and collaborative clinicians to provide outpatient PT services to patients in their homes in the Newton area (and other nearby locations). This is outpatient therapy delivered to the patient's home, NOT typical home health. As a physical therapist, your job is fully centered around helping others. Often we find that PTs feel burnout and exhaustion from their career path. There has to be a better way, and it takes technology, community, and equitable compensation. 

We offer autonomy, flexibility, & work-life balance
Therapists succeed when they are given the freedom and independence to do what they do best. That’s why our model allows you to choose when you want to see patients during any hours/days of the week. Luna enables you to decide. Do you need a full schedule of patients, or just to supplement your standing clinic hours? Luna offers the platform to create just what you need. 

We provide competitive compensation, Concierge service, and technology you won’t get anywhere else
Alongside competitive compensation, regular bonuses, and instant payment, we've developed support and technology with the physical therapist's well-being at the top of our mind. From safety, cancellations, and billing, to finding the patient home or facility; unique issues can make the independent therapist's job more challenging than expected. 

\n

Built for PTs to restore their well-being 
No productivity standards
More work = more earnings
Enjoy increased autonomy with unmatched support 
Eliminate burnout and reclaim your love for the profession!

Reimagining a tech-enabled physical therapy experience 
Luna Auto-Charting automates the time-consuming process of documentation
Luna's Safety Shield for a convenient and safe experience for our PTs 
Luna Exercises to deliver an integrated care experience by prescribing and monitoring workouts via the Luna app
Geofencing technology to match patients based on your specialty and location (within 30 minute drive) 
Instantly receive payments that are direct deposited and visible in-app!

Job Requirements
State-specific PT license
Reliable, personal transportation for therapists practicing outside city centers
1+ years of experience
Liability insurance
CPR/BLS

Join the Lunaverse!
Job Types: Part-time, Contract, Per diem
Benefits: Flexible Scheduling, Referral Program
Medical specialties: Geriatrics, Home Health, Neurology, Orthopedics, Physical & Rehabilitation Medicine, Sports Medicine
Schedule: Choose your own hours
Work Location: On the road

\n
$70 - $75 an hour
In addition to regular bonuses. 

\n
Luna Values

Care Exceptionally 
We value safety and trust even when mistakes occur. We prioritize open and purposeful communication, addressing concerns early and often. We apply creative and innovative efforts aimed at enabling the success of our patients, therapists, physicians, partners, and team members.
Incredibly Relentless 
We prioritize persistence even over immediate success. We understand that continuous effort often leads to greater long-term achievements. We empower each other to make decisions, and approach our goals deliberately and holistically.
Be Impactful
We prioritize fewer high-impact goals over lots of low-impact goals. We are decisive, yet keep an open-mind. We assume positive intent, and hold one another accountable. We know that the details matter, and take ownership in solutions that prioritize the patient and therapist experience.
Become 1% Better, Every Day
We value continual growth over the professional "comfort zone." We have a culture of curiosity and an obsession for constant improvement. We believe there's always a better way to do things. We celebrate our successes and learn from our failures.
ai_core_responsibilities: Provide outpatient physical therapy services to patients in their homes. Focus on helping patients while maintaining therapist well-being and avoiding burnout.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a state-specific PT license, reliable transportation, and at least 1 year of experience. Liability insurance and CPR/BLS certification are also required.

title: Associate Attorney
description_text:
A growing mid-size law firm is seeking Associate Attorneys to support an expanding caseload within a high-volume practice environment. The firm is focused on delivering effective legal representation and achieving strong outcomes for clients through structured case management, collaboration, and consistent communication.

This role supports matters involving coordination with clients, medical providers, and insurance entities. Attorneys with 2–3+ years of experience are preferred, though candidates with transferable legal experience are encouraged to apply. Prior experience in personal injury law is beneficial but not required.
\n

Responsibilities
Monitor client treatment and case progression
Communicate with insurance companies and medical providers
Support case approvals and provide secondary-level client communication
Assist with negotiation and resolution of claims
Maintain organized and accurate case documentation
Manage multiple cases while meeting internal timelines
Collaborate with attorneys and support staff to advance case outcomes

Qualifications
Active North Carolina State Bar license in good standing
2–3+ years of legal experience
Strong organizational and time-management skills
Ability to work independently and in a team-based environment
Effective written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and commitment to quality work
Experience in personal injury or related practice areas
Experience negotiating with insurance carriers or third parties

\n
$80,000 - $95,000 a year
\n
#LAT
ai_core_responsibilities: The Associate Attorney will monitor client treatment and case progression, communicate with insurance companies and medical providers, and assist with negotiation and resolution of claims. They will also maintain organized case documentation and manage multiple cases while collaborating with attorneys and support staff.

Strong organizational skills, effective communication abilities, and attention to detail are essential, with personal injury law experience being beneficial but not required.

title: Test Engineer (DoD Clearance Required)
description_text:
North Point Technology is seeking a Test Engineer to support a mission-critical program delivering intelligence and geospatial data management applications. This program supports current operational mission needs while developing future automation capabilities aligned with agency directives. The work contributes to advanced geospatial analysis and production solutions that enhance the distribution of intelligence to stakeholders.
\n

Responsibilities 
Perform integration, system, formal acceptance, and independent verification and validation testing
Develop and execute test plans, test procedures, and formal acceptance tests
Generate feature acceptance criteria and requirement verification criteria
Coordinate testing activities across multiple functional teams, contractors, and locations
Support operational baselines, patch delivery, and operational checkout activities
Participate in enterprise testing efforts, including coordination with external organizations
Document test results and support issue identification and resolution

Qualifications
Experience with integration testing, system testing, formal acceptance testing, and independent verification and validation
Experience coordinating across multiple functional disciplines, contractors, and locations
Experience developing test plans, test procedures, and acceptance criteria
Experience supporting operational baselines, patch delivery, and operational checkout
Experience writing and executing formal acceptance tests
Experience supporting enterprise testing with external organizations
Experience with automated testing tools (e.g., JMeter, Selenium)
Experience with automated test scripting (e.g., C#, Perl)
Experience with software unit and component testing
Experience using version control systems (e.g., Git)
Experience working in SAFe Agile development environments and scrum teams
Experience with requirements-based testing
Knowledge of or experience supporting CAOS or NES/IESS mission applications
Strong communication, organization, and planning skills
Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and project tracking tools
Experience supporting GEOINT systems or programs

\n
$125,000 - $180,000 a year

\n
#LAT
